title: MCP Server Integration
description: Developer connects MCP servers; agent discovers and calls external tools via MCP protocol

use_cases.name: Connect MCP Server via Stdio
use_cases.description: Instantiate MCPServerStdio with command/args params; SDK spawns subprocess and opens stdio pipes via stdio_client()

use_cases.name: Connect MCP Server via SSE
use_cases.description: Instantiate MCPServerSse with URL params; SDK connects over HTTP with Server-Sent Events (deprecated, legacy support)

use_cases.name: Connect MCP Server via Streamable HTTP
use_cases.description: Instantiate MCPServerStreamableHttp with URL/headers/auth params; SDK opens Streamable HTTP transport with session management

use_cases.name: Manage Multiple MCP Server Lifecycles
use_cases.description: Use MCPServerManager as async context manager to connect multiple servers in parallel or series; failed servers tracked in failed_servers/errors; active_servers exposed to agent

use_cases.name: Use Hosted MCP Tool
use_cases.description: Add HostedMCPTool to agent tools list; OpenAI Responses API handles tool discovery and invocation remotely without local connection

use_cases.name: Discover Available Tools
use_cases.description: Agent runner calls MCPUtil.get_all_function_tools() which calls list_tools() on each connected MCPServer; results converted to FunctionTool objects

use_cases.name: Filter Available Tools
use_cases.description: Static filter: allowlist/blocklist by tool name. Dynamic filter: callable (ToolFilterContext, MCPTool) -> bool. Applied during list_tools()

use_cases.name: Cache Tool List
use_cases.description: Set cache_tools_list=True on server constructor; first call fetches from server, subsequent calls return cached list. Invalidate via invalidate_tools_cache()
key_methods: list_tools() checks: cache_tools_list and not _cache_dirty and _tools_list

use_cases.name: Convert MCP Tool to Function Tool
use_cases.description: MCPUtil.to_function_tool() wraps MCPTool into FunctionTool; resolves schema strictness, approval policy, error handler, tool origin metadata

use_cases.name: Invoke MCP Tool
use_cases.description: Agent calls MCPUtil.invoke_mcp_tool(); resolves _meta via tool_meta_resolver, merges static/dynamic meta, calls server.call_tool(tool_name, arguments, meta)

use_cases.name: Require Tool Approval (Human-in-the-Loop)
use_cases.description: Set require_approval on server (always/never/dict/callable/bool); normalized to needs_approval policy; FunctionTool.needs_approval is set accordingly; agent pauses for approval before execution

use_cases.name: Inject Per-Call Metadata
use_cases.description: Provide tool_meta_resolver callable on server; invoked before each call_tool with MCPToolMetaContext(run_context, server_name, tool_name, arguments) -> dict | None

use_cases.name: Retrieve Prompts from MCP Server
use_cases.description: Call list_prompts() for enumeration, get_prompt(name, arguments) to fetch a concrete prompt with parameters for use as agent instructions

use_cases.name: Read Resources from MCP Server
use_cases.description: Call list_resources(cursor), list_resource_templates(cursor), or read_resource(uri) to retrieve external data resources exposed by the MCP server

use_cases.name: Retry Failed Tool Calls
use_cases.description: Set max_retry_attempts > 0 on server; list_tools() and call_tool() automatically retry with exponential backoff on failure

use_cases.name: Reconnect Failed MCP Servers
use_cases.description: Call MCPServerManager.reconnect(failed_only=True) to retry failed servers only, or failed_only=False to cleanup and restart all servers

class_hierarchy: _ServerWorker wraps MCPServer (for parallel connect/cleanup in MCPServerManager)
key_relationships: MCPServerManager exposes active_servers: list[MCPServer] to Agent
_MCPServerWithClientSession holds ClientSession (MCP SDK) for protocol communication
ToolFilter (static dict or callable) attached to _MCPServerWithClientSession
MCPToolMetaResolver callable attached to MCPServer, invoked per call_tool
RequireApprovalSetting on MCPServer normalizes to needs_approval policy on FunctionTool

transport_types.MCPServerStdio: Spawns local subprocess; communicates over stdin/stdout
transport_types.MCPServerStreamableHttp: Streamable HTTP; supports session IDs, retries, auth
transport_types.HostedMCPTool: OpenAI Responses API hosted; no local connection; model calls remote server

important_notes: MCPServer.connect() must be called before list_tools() or call_tool(); enforced with UserError
MCPServerManager.active_servers only contains successfully connected servers when drop_failed_servers=True (default)
Tool name uniqueness enforced across all MCP servers in MCPUtil.get_all_function_tools()
Static tool filter: allowed_tool_names applied first, then blocked_tool_names removed
Dynamic tool filter requires run_context and agent; errors cause tool exclusion (fail-safe)
HostedMCPTool: added to agent.tools (not mcp_servers); tool round-trip handled by OpenAI
MCPServer.use_structured_content: if True, prefers tool_result.structured_content over content list
list_tools() traced with mcp_tools_span(); call_tool() traced with FunctionSpanData.mcp_data
connect_in_parallel in MCPServerManager uses _ServerWorker tasks to preserve task affinity for AnyIO cancel scopes
